title: UN Sustainable Development Goal Alignment
note: SDG alignment is easy to overclaim — every startup maps itself to half the goals. This file distinguishes goals the product GENUINELY and DIRECTLY serves from ones it touches tangentially, and it flags where the product's second-order effects could run AGAINST a goal. An honest SDG map includes the tensions.

phase_context.statement: AquaVision is now three layers, and the SDG story differs by layer. Phase 1 (free app: guided setup, AI assistant, water-quality logging, disease ID, learning modules, community) is the mechanism that reaches smallholders who previously had NO guidance at all — this is where SDG 1, 2, and 4 land hardest, and it does so without the affordability paradox that constrains the hardware layer. Phase 2 (premium subscription, marketplace, expert consultations) is where SDG 8/9 livelihood effects strengthen as farms convert to paid tools. Phase 3 (OmniDrone/IoT/PondTwin, enterprise) is the layer this file's ORIGINAL analysis was written for — SDG 14 (depth-resolved water monitoring) and the environmental tensions below still apply there, but Phase 3 is priced for commercial/enterprise operators, so its equity contribution is weaker than Phase 1's.
phase_context.honest_caveat: Do not let the free Phase 1 tier launder the Phase 3 equity problem. A smallholder using the free app for water-quality logging is not the same as a smallholder benefiting from drone-based depth monitoring — those remain two different products with two different reach profiles.

primary_alignment.title: Zero Hunger
primary_alignment.target: 2.4 — sustainable food production systems and resilient agricultural practices
primary_alignment.mechanism: Aquatic food is a major and growing protein source; reducing losses and improving yield in aquaculture directly increases food availability. Aquaculture is now the majority of aquatic animal production.
primary_alignment.honest_caveat: The contribution is real but bounded by our addressable fraction of the sector and by whether farms act on the information. Do not claim to 'solve hunger'.

primary_alignment.title: Life Below Water
primary_alignment.target: 14.2 — sustainably manage and protect marine and coastal ecosystems; 14.1 — reduce marine pollution
primary_alignment.mechanism: Measuring aquaculture's effluent load and supporting carrying-capacity management reduces the eutrophication of shared water bodies. Pollution detection supports 14.1.
primary_alignment.honest_caveat: The strongest environmental claim, BUT it depends on the carrying-capacity use case actually being adopted, which requires cross-farm data-sharing that is unresolved (see ethics-data-privacy.json). And note the over-stocking paradox below — the product could also enable intensification.

primary_alignment.title: Responsible Consumption and Production
primary_alignment.target: 12.2 — sustainable management and efficient use of natural resources
primary_alignment.mechanism: Reducing feed waste (feed is ~60 percent of operating cost and a major environmental load) improves resource efficiency. Precision dosing reduces chemical and antimicrobial use.
primary_alignment.honest_caveat: The antimicrobial claim cuts both ways — easier dosing can mean more dosing. See the tension below. Note: at Phase 1/2 this mechanism runs through farmer behaviour change (better feeding decisions from logged data and AI guidance), not sensor precision — a softer but much more widely reachable version of the same claim.

primary_alignment.title: Quality Education
primary_alignment.target: 4.4 — increase the number of people with relevant skills for decent work and entrepreneurship; 4.7 — knowledge and skills needed to promote sustainable development
primary_alignment.mechanism: The Phase 1 free app is, functionally, an extension service delivered as software: an AI-assisted fish-farming guide, structured learning modules, photo-based disease identification, and a farmer community — reaching beginners and smallholders who had no prior access to aquaculture agronomy guidance at all. This is a NEW primary alignment created by the phased pivot, not present in the old hardware-only story.
primary_alignment.honest_caveat: Educational reach is not the same as educational EFFECT. An AI assistant answering questions is not equivalent to extension-agent contact, and 'learning module completed' is an activity metric, not a competency or outcome metric. See measuring_impact_honestly in social-economic-impact.json — this claim needs the same counterfactual discipline as the others, not an exemption because it feels more benign.

secondary_alignment.title: No Poverty
secondary_alignment.mechanism: For smallholders, a single kill event can be financially catastrophic. Preventing or mitigating it protects livelihoods. Continuous records could unlock credit and insurance for the currently uninsurable. Critically, the Phase 1 free tier now gives this population basic record-keeping, feeding guidance, and disease-ID alerting even before any hardware or paid tier is involved — resolving, for this specific mechanism, the affordability barrier the old hardware-only story could not.
secondary_alignment.honest_caveat: The credit/insurance upside and the catastrophic-kill-prevention upside from Phase 3 depth monitoring still depend entirely on cooperative/institutional channels and remain unresolved by affordability. The Phase 1 free-tier contribution is real but narrower: better everyday decisions and record continuity, not depth-resolved early warning. Don't conflate the two.

secondary_alignment.title: Decent Work and Economic Growth
secondary_alignment.mechanism: Removes the dangerous pre-dawn boat run (a real drowning risk), and stabilises aquaculture incomes (SDG 8.8 — safe working environments). At Phase 2, the marketplace and paid expert consultations create a more direct livelihood channel: better input prices/access via the marketplace, and professional advice smallholders previously could not reach or afford individually.
secondary_alignment.honest_note: The occupational-safety angle is genuine and underused, and it is specifically a Phase 3 hardware benefit. It is a human argument, not just an efficiency one. The marketplace/consultation livelihood angle is Phase 2 and unproven — no transaction volume exists yet (see kpi-definitions.json phase_1_2_saas_kpis, all awaiting_data).

secondary_alignment.title: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
secondary_alignment.mechanism: Brings precision-agriculture-grade instrumentation to a sector and geography that lacks it (Phase 3), and, earlier and more broadly, brings basic digital record-keeping and data-driven farm management tools to smallholders who currently run entirely on paper or memory (Phase 1/2).
secondary_alignment.honest_caveat: The Phase 1/2 infrastructure contribution is software adoption, which is a much lower bar than the Phase 3 instrumentation claim — worth stating plainly rather than letting the two blur together.

secondary_alignment.title: Climate Action
secondary_alignment.mechanism: Increasing frequency of thermal and rainfall extremes worsens fish kills; the platform shortens reaction time to climate-driven events. Adaptation, not mitigation.
secondary_alignment.honest_caveat: We do not reduce emissions. We help farmers adapt to climate volatility's local expression. Claiming climate MITIGATION would be false.

the_tensions_an_honest_sdg_map_must_incl.note: This section is what separates a real SDG analysis from a marketing exercise. Every one of these is a way the product could work AGAINST a goal it claims to serve.

tensions.detail: Better instruments may let operators push stocking density higher, worsening the eutrophication and carrying-capacity problems the product claims to help. A tool sold on environmental stewardship could enable environmental degradation.
tensions.our_response: Carrying-capacity analytics aimed at collective governance; not marketing as a licence to intensify. Partial mitigation only. See risk R-15.

tensions.detail: Easier dosing can mean more prophylactic antimicrobial use, contributing to antimicrobial resistance — a serious public health problem.
tensions.our_response: Dose ceilings, human authorisation, precision placement to reduce total dose. Weak controls against a determined operator. See ethics-data-privacy.json.

tensions.detail: The phased model changes but does not resolve this tension. Phase 1 (free) genuinely opens the door to smallholders who could never afford the old hardware-first product. But Phase 2's premium tier and Phase 3's enterprise hardware are still priced for farms that have already outgrown manual tracking — so the platform could still stratify: smallholders permanently on the free tier while capitalised farms buy their way to predictive analytics, marketplace leverage, and drone-based monitoring. A three-tier product can encode inequality just as easily as a single expensive product can, only with a free layer as cover.
tensions.our_response: Cooperative and institutional channels remain the mechanism for equitable reach INTO Phase 2/3, and the free Phase 1 tier is itself a genuine, if partial, equity mechanism it did not have before. Neither is automatic. Whether Phase 1 usage among smallholders actually converts into better outcomes — not just into a large free user base that subsidises paid customers — is unmeasured. See kpi-smallholder-reach in kpi-definitions.json.

tensions.detail: Aerial imagery over communities, and the latent surveillance capability, could harm the people the product is meant to serve.
tensions.our_response: No person-detection, no enforcement use, data-minimisation. See ethics-data-privacy.json.

tensions.tension: Environmental footprint of the hardware (against SDG 12)
tensions.detail: Lithium batteries, electronic waste, and the risk of a ditched drone contaminating a food-production water body are real environmental costs of the solution itself.
tensions.our_response: Lithium containment, ditching provision, and honest lifecycle accounting. A product sold on stewardship must account for its own footprint. See environmental-impact.json.

the_tensions_an_honest_sdg_map_must_incl.why_this_matters: An SDG map with only benefits is a sustainability-washing exercise, and sophisticated impact investors and thesis committees recognise it instantly. Including the tensions — with honest, sometimes-partial responses — is what makes the alignment credible.

how_to_use_this.for_impact_investors: Lead with SDG 2, 14, 12, and now 4 (the genuine primary alignments — 4 specifically for the Phase 1/2 free-education story), then volunteer the tensions. An impact investor who sees you have thought about the over-stocking paradox AND the free-tier-as-cover-for-stratification risk trusts the rest of your claims.
how_to_use_this.for_the_thesis: This is the significance-of-the-study section. The tensions belong in the discussion chapter as limitations, not hidden.
how_to_use_this.the_discipline: Never map the product to a goal it does not directly serve just to fill the grid. Four strong alignments plus honest tensions beats seventeen weak ones. Adding SDG 4 because Phase 1 genuinely does deliver education-shaped value is legitimate; do not let the phased pivot become an excuse to add SDGs 10, 5, or others the product does not directly serve.
